judge_verdict.reasoning: Only two tools produced meaningful outputs: history_search and text_reasoning. The text_reasoning tool analyzed a chest X-ray report showing bilateral lower lobe opacities consistent with pneumonia, a small left pleural effusion, and normal cardiomediastinal silhouette, with 95% confidence. The history_search returned prior records related to a left middle finger injury in a 14-year-old male, which is clinically unrelated to the current chest findings. There is no contradiction between these tools — the history simply shows no prior respiratory conditions, which is consistent with the text_reasoning assessment of a new-onset pulmonary process. The synthesis correctly integrates both findings, noting the prior history is unrelated and that this appears to be a new acute process. The plan follows standard clinical guidelines for community-acquired pneumonia (clinical correlation, empiric antibiotics, monitoring, follow-up imaging). No image analysis tool was run, but the text_reasoning was performed on the radiology report itself, which provides adequate basis for assessment. No genuine clinical disagreements exist between tool outputs.
low_confidence_items: Prior AI report for left middle finger injury had only 50% confidence, though this is unrelated to the current presentation
